review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the control SC-7(21) focusing on boundary protection. The use of NetworkPolicies for ingress and egress restrictions is appropriate. The function 'k8s_service_type_not_nodeport' correctly identifies a potential boundary protection risk by avoiding NodePort services, which can expose services to the public internet. The function 'k8s_ingress_tls_enabled' ensures secure communication, which is crucial for boundary protection. All function names follow the naming conventions and clearly describe the checks being performed.
title: Boundary Protection - Boundary protection | isolation of system components employ boundary protection
step1_reasoning: The control SC-7(21) focuses on boundary protection and isolation of system components, which directly maps to Kubernetes network security practices. In Kubernetes, NetworkPolicies can be used to enforce ingress and egress restrictions, ensuring that pods cannot communicate with unauthorized external or internal endpoints. Additionally, using service types like NodePort can expose services to the public internet, so restricting this is crucial for boundary protection. Enabling TLS on Ingress controllers ensures secure communication, further isolating and protecting system components.
